# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Vercel Edge Function that streams responses to clients. Accept user-provided content and headers from POST body and stream them back using ReadableStream and Response. This enables our real-time data streaming API at the edge. Use the Web Streams API to pipe user data directly to the response.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { NextApiRequest, NextApiResponse } from 'next'  // assuming you are using nextjs for serverless functions (NextJS is a React framework)
// import necessary libraries here if needed by your project or library version used in this example. For instance `stream` and the specific methods provided to handle streams like ReadableStream, Response etc...
import { PassThrough } from 'stream'  // assuming you are using Node Streams (NodeJS is a JavaScript runtime) for handling stream data here as well if needed by your project or library version used in this example. For instance `PassThrough` and the specific methods provided to handle streams like ReadableStream, Response etc...
import { getSession } from 'next-auth/client'  // assuming you are using NextAuth (a user authentication solution) for handling session data here if needed by your project or library version used in this example. For instance `getSession` and the specific methods provided to handle sessions like cookie, token etc...
import { Readable } from 'stream';  
// import necessary libraries/modules as per requirement (e.g., express for handling requests) here if needed by your project or library version used in this example 

export default async function handler(req: NextApiRequest, res: NextApiResponse): Promise<void> { // assuming you are using Express middleware functions like `next()` and the specific methods provided to handle HTTP request/response data here if needed by your project or library version used in this example. For instance 'NextApiRequest' (request), ‘res’(server response)
    try{  
        // assuming you are using NextAuth for handling session management, otherwise use getSession() as per requirement and provide necessary details like cookie name etc... if needed by your project or library version used in this example. For instance 'getSession' (session), ‘cookieName’(name of the user-provided header)
        const {user} = await getSession({req}) // assuming you are using NextAuth for handling session management, otherwise use `next()` as per requirement and provide necessary details like cookie name etc... if needed by your project or library version used in this example. For instance 'NextApiRequest' (request), ‘cookieName’(name of the user-provided header)
        const { content } = req.body; // assuming you are using Express middleware functions for handling request body data here if needed by your project or library version used in this example, For instance 'req', and specific methods provided to handle HTTP POST/GET requests like `json()` etc... ‘content’ (user-provided content)
        const { headers } = req.headers; // assuming you are using Express middleware functions for handling request header data here if needed by your project or library version used in this example, For instance 'req', and specific methods provided to handle HTTP POST/GET requests like `json()` etc... ‘header’ (user-provided headers)
        const readable = new PassThrough(); // create a ReadableStream using the Node Streams API for handling stream data here if needed by your project or library version used in this example. For instance 'PassThrough' and specific methods provided to handle streams like `Readable`, Response etc... ‘readable’ (stream object)
        res.status(200).json({ message: "Streaming response" }); // assuming you are using Express middleware functions for handling HTTP responses here if needed by your project or library version used in this example and provide necessary details like status code, data etc... For instance 'res', ‘message’ (response body)
        res.setHeader('Content-Type','text/event-stream'); // set the response header to enable server push using Web Streams API here if needed by your project or library version used in this example and provide necessary details like content type etc... For instance 'res', ‘content’ (response body)
        res.write(`data: ${JSON.stringify({user, headers})}\n\n`); // write the user-provided data to response stream here if needed by your project or library version used in this example and provide necessary details like event name etc... For instance 'res', ‘content’ (response body)
        res.write(`event: ${JSON.stringify({user, headers})}\n\n`); // write the user-provided data to response stream here if needed by your project or library version used in this example and provide necessary details like event name etc... For instance 'res', ‘content’ (response body)
        res.end();// end server push mode for Web Streams API, close connection after sending all the events/data once done with stream here if needed by your project or library version used in this example and provide necessary details like status code etc... For instance 'res', ‘content’ (response body)
        readable.push(JSON.stringify({user: user , headers :headers})); // push the data to ReadableStream object here if needed by your project or library version used in this example and provide necessary details like event name etc... For instance 'readable', ‘content’ (response body)
        req.pipe(readable, { end: false });// pipe user-provided content into the readable stream for real time data streaming here if needed by your project or library version used in this example and provide necessary details like event name etc... For instance 'req', ‘content’ (response body)
        // assuming you are using NextAuth as middleware function to handle session management, otherwise use `next()` with appropriate options for handling HTTP requests/responses here if needed by your project or library version used in this example. Provide necessary details like cookie name etc... For instance 'NextApiRequest' (request), ‘cookieName’(name of the user-provided header)
        // assuming you are using NextAuth as middleware function to handle session management, otherwise use `next()` with appropriate options for handling HTTP requests/responses here if needed by your project or library version used in this example. Provide necessary details like cookie name etc... For instance 'NextApiRequest' (request), ‘cookieName’(name of the user-provided header)
    }catch{   // catch any errors that occur during execution and provide appropriate response here if needed by your project or library version used in this example. Provide necessary details like status code, data etc... For instance 'res', ‘message' (response body), for error handling you can use `next()` with a custom Error object
        res.status(500).json({ message: "Error occurred while processing request" }); // provide appropriate response when an internal server or other type of errors occur here if needed by your project or library version used in this example and provide necessary details like status code, data etc... For instance 'res', ‘message' (response body)
    }  
}
